title: DSDS Link Definition
description: A typed pointer to an external resource — source code, design files, docs, packages. Every link needs a `kind` and a `url`. Links are pointers only: inter-entity references are expressed as typed edges in the entity's `relationships` array, never as links. (The pre-0.14 identifier-bearing link form was removed in the 0.14.0 breaking window; `scripts/migrate-relationship-links.js` converts old documents.)

link.description: A typed pointer to an external resource, addressed by `url`. The `kind` property sorts the reference. Standard kinds: 'source', 'design', 'storybook', 'documentation', 'package', 'repository', 'manifest' (a machine-readable component manifest, e.g. a Custom Elements Manifest). Custom values are allowed. For how entities relate to each other, use the entity's `relationships` array — it carries typed, validated edges; links are for resources outside the document.

kind.description: The category of the linked resource. Standard kinds: 'source', 'design', 'storybook', 'documentation', 'package', 'repository', 'manifest'. Custom values are allowed and MUST match ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$ (schema-enforced; all standard kinds already do).

url.description: The URL of the linked resource. MUST be a valid absolute URI.

label.description: Display text for this link — what a user sees when the link renders (e.g., 'React component source', 'Design file — variants'). When omitted, tools MAY build a label from the URL.
